steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text says the microchip “radically altered” the semiconductor industry, and historians argue it had a fundamental effect by enabling mass production for the first time. The blank needs a word meaning a deep, enabling change — one that made the industry capable of something it could not do before.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — “overwhelmed”: Means overpowered or defeated. The text frames the microchip’s effect as positive and enabling (mass production became possible), not as something that crushed or defeated the industry. ✗
  • Choice B — “bypassed”: Means intentionally avoided or circumvented. The microchip directly affected the semiconductor industry; it did not go around it. ✗
  • Choice C — “obstructed”: Means blocked or hindered. Again, the effect described is positive — enabling mass production — not a hindrance. ✗
  • Choice D — “transformed”: Means substantially changed in character or capability. Matches the passage: the microchip fundamentally changed what the industry could do by unlocking mass production methods for the first time. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
